description: cdx-bmt-1 inclusion-proof verification, including must-NOT-verify cases: the legacy duplicate-odd root (CVE-2012-2459 pattern) and an untagged fold value.
oracle: Expected values were computed by an INDEPENDENT Python oracle (json + hashlib), not by the library under test, so a serializer or folding bug is caught rather than snapshotted.

vectors.name: a-in-two-leaf-tree
vectors.description: The minimal proof: one right sibling folds the leaf to the root.

vectors.name: c-in-three-leaf-tree-promoted-single-element-path
vectors.description: A promoted leaf contributes no sibling at the level it was promoted through, so its path is shorter than ceil(log2(n)).

vectors.name: a-in-three-leaf-tree
vectors.description: A leaf beneath a promotion still folds through every level at which it was actually paired.

vectors.name: e-in-five-leaf-tree-double-promotion-single-element-path
vectors.description: Double promotion: E pairs only at the top level, so its whole path is a single element.

vectors.name: position-flip-must-fail
vectors.description: Sibling position is load-bearing: folding a right sibling on the left yields a different root and MUST NOT verify.

vectors.name: legacy-duplicate-odd-root-must-not-verify-cve-2012-2459-pattern
vectors.description: The legacy untagged duplicate-odd root MUST NOT verify under cdx-bmt-1 — the construction change is detectable rather than silently compatible.

vectors.name: untagged-fold-value-must-not-verify-as-a-cdx-bmt-1-root
vectors.description: An untagged fold value (what the section 6.5 aggregated construction produces from the same inputs) MUST NOT verify as a cdx-bmt-1 root: the two folds are deliberately decoupled.
